source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


WHY CASCA? 

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do. We were founded by a world-class team of banking & AI experts from Stanford, MIT, and Y Combinator. We like to win—and we know that the only thing standing between us and the title is our own ability to improve every day.



You’ll be the talent magnet that powers our technical team. As our first technical recruiter, you'll construct our technical hiring engine from zero to hero.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Architect our technical recruiting strategy

 - Partner with founders and leadership team to define and hunt for A+ engineering talent

 - Create a candidate experience so good, even rejected applicants become fans

 - Build scalable processes that can handle 10x growth

 - Source, screen, and close exceptional engineers when the stakes are highest




WHO YOU ARE

 - A technical talent hunter with 3-5+ years catching engineering unicorns

 - Someone who's built recruiting functions from the ground up

 - Connected enough to have engineering leaders in your speed dial

 - Technical enough to evaluate skills without needing an interpreter

 - Scrappy, resourceful, and unfazed by ambiguity and rapid change




THE REWARD

 - A seat on the rocket ship with massive potential

 - The power to shape our company's DNA through the people you hire

 - Competitive salary and full benefits

 - Visa sponsorship

 - Direct impact on our success that you can point to for years



This is building something that matters.



Ready to be our talent multiplier?


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech.

As an early-stage company building at the frontier of AI, we work with high intensity and commitment. While schedules can vary by role/team, many weeks will demand extra focus, flexibility and time particularly during major launches and high impact sprints. We're seeking those who are aligned to and able to commit to that expectation.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.



You'll own a product vertical end-to-end. You'll work directly with our engineering team and bank customers to turn complex lending workflows into elegant, AI-native experiences. You'll make consequential decisions about what we build and why.

Our bottleneck isn't demand. It's delivery speed. Banks keep pulling us into new areas faster than we can build. We need people who can own big problems, work directly with sophisticated customers, and help us ship faster.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Define product strategy for your vertical. Make hard prioritization calls. Shape the roadmap based on customer needs, business impact, and technical feasibility.

 - Spend time with bank customers. Understand their workflows, pain points, and the regulatory constraints they operate within. Build products that actually solve problems.

 - Work closely with engineers to make good tradeoffs between speed, quality, and scope. Understand technical complexity.

 - Own the full lifecycle: requirements, development, QA, rollout, iteration. Measure what matters. Fix what doesn't work.

 - Move fast. Juggle multiple priorities, make decisions with incomplete information, adapt quickly as we learn.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years in product management in fast-paced, tech-driven environments (we're especially excited by prior startup experience)

 - Track record of shipping customer-facing products that users actually use

 - Strong analytical skills. You use data to make decisions, not to justify them after the fact.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication. You can explain complex ideas simply.

 - Experience with AI/ML products, financial services, or enterprise software

 - Exposure to diversity sourcing strategies and inclusive hiring practices.


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech.

As an early-stage company building at the frontier of AI, we work with high intensity and commitment. While schedules can vary by role/team, many weeks will demand extra focus, flexibility and time particularly during major launches and high impact sprints. We're seeking those who are aligned to and able to commit to that expectation.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Projected division of responsibilities: roughly 70% building and shipping, 30% embedded with customers and collaborating with your team.



Live in the Customer’s World

 - Embed directly with bank teams on-site, working alongside underwriters, lenders, and operators

 - Develop a deep understanding of their day-to-day workflows, bottlenecks, and pain points

 - Become the person who understands these processes better than anyone because you have seen them firsthand



Build Something Magical

 - Turn your understanding into working software that automates and improves workflows

 - Build on top of Casca’s platform to extend it into new use cases

 - Deploy AI-powered systems that replace manual processes

 - Iterate quickly based on real user feedback in production

 - Help migrate customers from legacy systems to modern, scalable solutions



Own the Lifecycle, and Shape the Platform

 - Take ownership end-to-end, from problem definition to deployment and iteration

 - Scope problems effectively and focus on delivering real value quickly

 - Feed what you learn in the field back into the platform, improving future deployments

 - Contribute to product direction based on real-world experience




WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - A skillset at the intersection of software development, business strategy and customer engagement

 - 1-6+ years of experience in software engineering

 - Proven track record of translating complex technical concepts into clear business value propositions with measurable outcomes

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with experience driving initiatives from concept to execution

 - Solid understanding of modern programming languages and (AI) frameworks (e.g., React, TypeScript, Next.js, Python, LangChain, Vercel AI-SDK, etc.)

 - Strong problem-solving skills and a knack for innovative thinking

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative team spirit
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech.

As an early-stage company building at the frontier of AI, we work with high intensity and commitment. While schedules can vary by role/team, many weeks will demand extra focus, flexibility and time particularly during major launches and high impact sprints. We're seeking those who are aligned to and able to commit to that expectation.

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.



Don't see the perfect fit?

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.

This is your chance to be the CEO's right hand during our most critical scaling phase. You'll be the glue that keeps our fast-moving team aligned and executing flawlessly as we build the future of lending. From streamlining internal processes to orchestrating cross-team initiatives, you'll tackle the challenges that determine whether we scale efficiently or break under rapid growth.

What you'll do:

 - Manage CEO priorities and special projects, ensuring critical initiatives stay on track while maintaining focus on core business objectives

 - Partner directly with the CEO on internal and external meetings and initiatives

 - Drive cross-functional alignment and communication, breaking down silos between teams and ensuring everyone is working toward shared goals

 - Lead internal projects that drive operational excellence, from implementing new systems to streamlining workflows across all teams

What you bring:

 - 3+ years of Product / Engineering roles at high-growth tech companies OR management consulting experience at a leading firm (McKinsey, Bain, BCG, etc.)

 - Proven ability to structure complex operational challenges, build scalable processes, and drive execution in fast-paced startup environments

 - Experience managing internal operations, team coordination, or chief of staff responsibilities at high-growth companies

 - Strong analytical skills with ability to work across both quantitative analysis and qualitative strategic thinking

 - Exceptional communication skills and ability to coordinate across all levels of the organization, from individual contributors to executive leadership

Bonus points:

 - Technical background with understanding of AI/ML applications

 - MBA from a top-tier program

 - Experience building internal operations during rapid team growth (30+ to 200+ employees)

 - Background in fintech, banking, or lending industry




WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech.

As an early-stage company building at the frontier of AI, we work with high intensity and commitment. While schedules can vary by role/team, many weeks will demand extra focus, flexibility and time particularly during major launches and high impact sprints. We're seeking those who are aligned to and able to commit to that expectation.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Projected Division of Responsibilities: 70% contributing code / 30% collaborating with stakeholders and mentoring

 - Lead Strategy with Urgency: Drive architectural decisions that support long-term stability while maintaining the pace required by a fast-growing banking platform. You will identify critical paths and make high-stakes technical calls to keep the organization moving efficiently.

 - Balance Coding & Leadership: Maintain a projected division of roughly 70% contributing high-quality code and 30% driving technical alignment. You will be expected to ship individually while unblocking the wider team.

 - Drive Execution: Lead the development of complex initiatives by chunking and sequencing work to maximize parallelization. You will be responsible for maintaining high throughput and ensuring features reach production without unnecessary delays.

 - Amplify the Team: Amplify your skills and vision by supporting the growth of the team. You will mentor team members both directly and by example, helping others reach their true potential and driving engineering best practices.

 - Optimize for Value: Leverage strong pattern recognition to build SaaS applications that effectively balance complexity, quality, and speed of delivery. You will build for our customers, avoiding complexity for complexity’s sake.

 - Elevate Standards: Lead initiatives to fine-tune controls and communication channels to improve predictability and system reliability. You will use data to ruthlessly prioritize work that moves the needle.

Our tech stack: TypeScript, React, NextJS, Postgres with Prisma, Python, AWS.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - Seasoned Engineering Expertise: 7+ years of software engineering experience with a track record of delivering significant projects in fast-paced, production SaaS environments. You have a holistic understanding of web architectures and are comfortable navigating a broad system.

 - Operational Resilience: You thrive in high-velocity environments and are comfortable making decisions under pressure. You understand that a perfect architecture is useless if it doesn't ship, and you know how to manage trade-offs to maintain momentum.

 - Architectural Maturity: A quality-first mentality, but with a bias for action. You can quickly identify the trade-offs between "doing it right" and "doing it now," and you know when to pull which lever.

 - Technical Mastery: Deep expertise in modern programming languages and web application frameworks, along with strong competence in data modeling for relational databases and distributed cloud platforms.

 - Security & Compliance Awareness: Experience in regulated industries (like Fintech or Healthcare) is a nice-to-have. We value engineers who instinctively design for security, reliability and compliance without slowing down execution.

 - Strategic Problem Solving: You can take a high-level business goal and independently drive it to a technical solution, navigating ambiguity and minimizing churn along the way.


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech.

As an early-stage company building at the frontier of AI, we work with high intensity and commitment. While schedules can vary by role/te

source_jobs.title: Senior Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


ROLE OVERVIEW

We're looking for a Senior Infrastructure Engineer to own and evolve the foundational systems that power Casca's AI-driven lending platform. You'll build and maintain the infrastructure that enables our engineering team to ship quickly and reliably—CI/CD pipelines, cloud infrastructure, deployment automation, and observability systems. You thrive on making other engineers more productive while ensuring our platform remains secure, compliant, and highly available.

 

This is a hands-on role. You'll write significant code, debug complex distributed systems issues, and drive technical decisions that impact the entire engineering organization.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, build, and maintain CI/CD pipelines and deployment automation that enable fast, safe releases across multiple environments.

 - Own infrastructure-as-code (AWS CDK, Terraform or Pulumi) for cloud resources across AWS and Cloudflare

 - Build and improve observability systems—metrics, logging, tracing, and alerting—that give engineers deep visibility into system behavior and enable rapid incident response.

 - Develop internal tooling and automation that eliminates toil and accelerates developer workflows.

 - Drive reliability improvements: capacity planning, failure mode analysis, disaster recovery, and incident response processes.

 - Collaborate with product engineers to design systems that are operationally sound from day one—balancing speed of delivery with long-term maintainability.

 - Contribute to security and compliance initiatives, ensuring infrastructure meets the requirements of a regulated fintech environment.

 


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 5+ years of experience in infrastructure, platform engineering, or SRE roles, with a track record of building systems that other engineers depend on daily.

 - Strong coding skills in at least one of: Python, Go, TypeScript, or Rust. You write production-quality automation, not just glue scripts.

 - Deep experience with cloud platforms (AWS preferred) and their core services—compute, networking, IAM, managed databases, and storage.

 - Proficiency with infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, CDK, Pulumi) and CI/CD systems (GitHub Actions, BuildKite, or similar).

 - Solid understanding of containerization (Docker) and container orchestration concepts, with hands-on experience running production workloads.

 - Experience building and operating observability stacks—you've instrumented systems, built dashboards, and used telemetry data to diagnose production issues.

 - Familiarity with common failure modes in distributed systems and strategies to mitigate them (blast radius control, graceful degradation, rollback mechanisms).

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where you'll need to balance "doing it right" with "shipping it now."

 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in regulated industries (fintech, healthcare) where security and compliance are first-class concerns.

 - Familiarity with ML pipeline orchestration.

 - Experience with multi-tenant SaaS architectures and customer-specific deployment requirements.

 - Contributions to on-call rotations and incident response in high-stakes production environments.


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the

source_jobs.title: Founding Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


The Mission

Casca was founded by a team of Banking IT experts and AI researchers from Stanford University, Georgia Tech, and Goldman Sachs who believe that the future of banking is AI-native. Backed by Canapi, Y Combinator and top 100 US Banks, we're revolutionizing the $7T lending industry through GenAI systems - replacing the lending software banks have relied on for 30 years



This is your chance to be inside the room when America's biggest banks switch from legacy to AI. Loan processes that took months will take minutes — and you'll be the one making it happen. Join us in building something unprecedented.



What you'll do

As a Deployment Specialist, you will help translate ambitious AI capabilities into production systems inside major U.S. banks — connecting business goals, technical design, and regulatory requirements to ensure bold ideas become reality. From initial planning through testing and launch, you'll bring order to complexity, drive clarity across stakeholder groups, and keep critical milestones on track. You'll be the voice of the client in day-to-day execution: getting embedded with client teams, understanding their workflows and constraints, and turning that into clear decisions for Casca.



Ship the Product

 - Deploy Casca's AI loan origination platform inside Tier 1 banks — you're on the ground when legacy systems that took decades to build get replaced in months

 - Drive technical delivery alongside Casca's engineering team: core banking integrations, API specifications, data mapping, environment setup — you're not coordinating from afar, you're in it

 - Own the project cadence — leading standups, tracking progress, testing software — you're the person who knows where every workstream actually stands

 - Flag slippage early, and surface risks before they compound



Evolve the Product

 - Gather and synthesize product requirements directly from bank users — understand how they work, what they expect, and where current capabilities fall short

 - Translate client needs into actionable feature requests, user stories, and prioritized input for Product and engineering

 - Surface edge cases and integration friction that only emerge in live deployments — your perspective shapes what gets built next



Be the Expert

 - Become the ground truth on your bank — their systems, data models, compliance requirements, and internal dynamics

 - Build deep domain knowledge in banking and lending — understand the regulatory, legacy, and organizational landscape well enough to anticipate problems before they surface



Own the Client Relationship

 - Earn buy-in at the working level — VPs, Directors, and team leads. Become the person the bank's working team calls when something's off — you're Casca's credibility on the ground

 - Make Casca's AI make sense to the people who have to live with it. Train & enable client teams so they can understand what Casca's platform does and trust what it delivers




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 2+ years management consulting experience at a leading firm (McKinsey, Bain, BCG, etc.) technical program management, or implementation/deployment experience at a top tech company or high-growth start-up

 - Comfortable running workstreams, structuring ambiguous requirements, and driving day-to-day coordination across technical and non-technical teams

 - Have worked in detail-heavy, multi-stakeholder environments and were the person people came to when things got stuck

 - Eager to build deep domain expertise in banking and AI

 - Outstanding communication and presentation abilities
   

Bonus points:

 - Background in fintech or lending industry

 - Experience with AI-related projects

 - Previous experience at a high-growth startup

 - MBA from a top-tier program


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opport

source_jobs.title: Engagement Lead
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


The Mission

Casca was founded by a team of Banking IT experts and AI researchers from Stanford University, Georgia Tech, and Goldman Sachs who believe that the future of banking is AI-native. Backed by Canapi, Y Combinator and top 100 US Banks, we're revolutionizing the $7T lending industry through GenAI systems - replacing the lending software banks have relied on for 30 years

This is your chance to be inside the room when America's biggest banks switch from legacy to AI. Loan processes that took months will take minutes — and you'll be the one making it happen. Join us in building something unprecedented.



What you'll do


As an Engagement Lead, you will translate ambitious AI capabilities into production systems inside major U.S. banks — owning executive alignment, delivery outcomes, expansion strategy, and risk management. You'll own the full arc of Casca's most critical client relationships — from first kickoff to go-live and beyond — serving as the strategic leader who keeps complex programs moving and turns successful deployments into expanding partnerships. You'll also turn what you learn in the field into what scales — working with Product to elevate recurring patterns into platform capabilities, and codifying lessons learned into a repeatable playbook that improves with every bank we onboard.

Ship the Product

 - Deploy Casca's AI loan origination platform inside Tier 1 banks — you own the outcome when legacy systems that took decades to build get replaced in months

 - Make the calls on scope, sequencing, and tradeoffs — what gets accelerated, what gets descoped, and what gets escalated to Casca leadership

 - Remove roadblocks across bank and Casca teams — surface risks early, kill blockers fast, and keep complex multi-stakeholder programs moving.

 - Own ambiguity — when stakeholders conflict or requirements are murky, you resolve it, not escalate it

 - Own derisking at the portfolio level — observe patterns across engagements and act before issues compound


Evolve the Product

 - Partner with Product and engineering to translate client patterns into platform decisions — shape what becomes core product, what remains bespoke, and what must evolve for us to scale

 - Identify recurring gaps and systemic friction across engagements and translate them into roadmap direction



Build the Playbook

 - You're not following a deployment methodology — you're writing it. The frameworks you create will scale across every bank we onboard

 - Codify lessons learned, set standards, and build a repeatable deployment model that improves with every engagement

 - Drive cross-functional coordination across engineering, compliance, legal, and bank leadership — turn what works into how we operate



Be the Client Wizard

 - Walk into rooms with senior banking executives as the authority on AI-powered lending — you'll know their systems, their pain points, and how to move them forward

 - Develop and drive the narrative around AI-native lending and digital transformation — help banks see what's possible and build conviction to get there

 - Navigate complex organizational dynamics and keep executive sponsorship strong throughout the engagement. Keep every stakeholder moving in the same direction, even when they don't agree on where that is



Build the Team

 - Mentor and develop the embedded Deployment Strategists working inside each bank engagement

 - Set the operating rhythm for the Casca side of each engagement — how your team communicates, escalates, and collaborates with engineering

 - Be the connective tissue between client teams and Casca leadership — you translate what's happening on the ground into decisions at the company level



What you bring

 - 4–6 years management consulting experience at a leading firm (McKinsey, Bain, BCG, etc.) or

source_jobs.title: Director of Customer Support
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Director of Customer Support to build and lead the support function that keeps our bank customers successful on the Casca platform. This is a founding leadership role: you’ll design the support org from the ground up, set the standard for how we interact with our customers, and ensure every banker who uses Casca feels like they have a world-class partner behind them.

Our customers are loan officers, credit analysts, and banking executives at some of the country’s most respected financial institutions. They’re trusting Casca to replace mission-critical legacy systems—so excellent, responsive, and knowledgeable support isn’t just nice to have. It’s the foundation of our business.

This is a hands-on role. You’ll personally handle escalations, build processes, hire your initial team, and work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, and Implementation to close the loop on customer feedback. You’ll report to the COO.

A core part of this role is designing automated workflows and communication systems that let us deliver fast, consistent, high-quality support without scaling headcount linearly. We’re an AI company—we expect our support function to be AI-native too.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build the customer support function from scratch—define the team structure, tools, SLAs, escalation paths, and knowledge base

 - Hire, train, and lead a high-performing support team as we scale from dozens to hundreds of bank customers

 - Own the end-to-end customer support experience across all channels (email, chat, phone, and in-product)

 - Establish and track key support metrics (CSAT, first response time, resolution time, ticket deflection) and use them to drive continuous improvement

 - Personally handle complex escalations and VIP customer issues, especially in the early days

 - Build self-service resources—help center, FAQs, in-app guidance—that reduce ticket volume and empower customers

 - Develop deep domain expertise in commercial lending, SBA programs, and banking operations to provide consultative support

 - Evaluate and implement support tooling (helpdesk, chatbot, AI-assisted triage) that scales with the business

 - Design and automate support workflows—ticket routing, status updates, SLA alerts, and follow-up sequences—so the team spends time on problems, not process

 - Build automated customer communication touchpoints (proactive health checks, incident notifications, etc.) that keep customers informed without manual effort

 - Leverage AI and automation to handle high-volume, low-complexity requests (password resets, status inquiries, documentation lookups) and free up the team for consultative, high-value interactions




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 8–10 years of experience in customer support or technical support, with at least 5 years managing a team

 - Experience building or significantly scaling a support function at a B2B SaaS or fintech company

 - Deep empathy for customers and a genuine obsession with delivering an exceptional support experience

 - Comfort operating in a fast-paced, ambiguous startup environment—you build the playbook, not follow one

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills; you can de-escalate a frustrated banking executive and write a clear process doc with equal ease

 - Analytical mindset—you use data to prioritize, make decisions, and measure the impact of your work

 - Technical aptitude: you can learn complex product workflows quickly and explain them clearly to non-technical users

 - Experience designing automated workflows and communication sequences using tools like Zapier, n8n, or native helpdesk automation

 - Bonus: experience in banking, lending, or financial services


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of

source_jobs.title: Head of Security & Compliance
source_jobs.description:
WHY CASCA?

Casca is building AGI for banking. We’re replacing decades-old legacy systems with AI-native technology that automates 90% of the manual work humans once had to do.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build security tooling & processes that engineers actually use. Create internal mechanisms for appsec, identity and access management, and threat detection that naturally integrate into how the team ships.

 - Manage, mentor, and grow our team of application security engineers. Mature our Secure SDLC, threat modeling, and vulnerability management processes to ensure our security posture matches our growing responsibility..

 - Secure the agent execution surface. Partner with Engineering and Product to establish robust security architecture for our AI-driven workflows, ensuring strict data privacy, mitigating AI-specific vulnerabilities, and maintaining safe agentic identity.

 - Drive customer trust. Partner with go-to-market and legal teams to support compliance and customer-driven initiatives. Own and expand our compliance roadmap (SOC 2, SOC 1, ISO 27001), while keeping guardrails pragmatic for a fast-paced startup.

 - Lead incident response and detection. Build the detection pipeline, act as the primary commander, and turn every event into systemic improvements.


WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - 5+ years in progressive security roles, with at least 2+ years at a B2B tech, fintech, or highly regulated SaaS company.

 - Strong fundamentals in secure SDLC, cloud security (AWS/GCP), Web security, and DevSecOps practices.

 - Ability to develop lightweight, durable security policies, access controls, and data governance frameworks. A track record of building "practical security, not checkbox theater."

 - Nice to have: Experience securing LLM usage for both coding and in product use cases, and mitigating risks specific to agentic systems (e.g., unauthorized actions taken by autonomous agents, prompt injection, and data poisoning)

 - Proven track record of owning SOC 2 Type II and/or ISO 27001 compliance.

 - You can review a penetration test, debate architecture with a lead engineer, and present to a bank's CISO…all in the same day

 - You’re comfortable with incident response - calm, methodical, and effective under pressure; experience leading incidents end to end & driving the fixes that follow.

 - You thrive in ambiguity, know how to ruthlessly prioritize fixes to eliminate the highest risks first, and understand the balance between security and business velocity.

 - Experience in fintech or banking


WHAT YOU'LL GET:

 - Impact & Ownership: A unique opportunity to shape the future of banking through AI, owning end-to-end product initiatives.

 - Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a talented and passionate team that values continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.

 - Competitive Compensation: Includes salary, benefits, and potential equity in a fast-growing startup.

 - Professional Growth: Access to resources and mentorship to expand your skill set, influence strategy, and accelerate your career.

 - Culture of Innovation: We encourage risk-taking, learning from failures, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in fintech.

As an early-stage company building at the frontier of AI, we work with high intensity and commitment. While schedules can vary by role/team, many weeks will demand extra focus, flexibility and time particularly during major launches and high impact sprints. We're seeking those who are aligned to and able to commit to that expectation.
